name: soc2
description: SOC 2 Trust Services Criteria rules — security, availability, processing integrity, confidentiality, privacy. Layer 2 of the four-layer rubric composition.

severity_rules.pattern: Authentication endpoint has no rate limiting, lockout policy, or anomaly-detection hook on failed-login events.
severity_rules.description: TSC CC6.1 requires logical access controls including protection against brute-force authentication attacks.
severity_rules.remediation: Add rate limiting and progressive lockout on failed authentication; route anomalies to security monitoring.

severity_rules.pattern: Sensitive endpoint accepts authentication via a long-lived static token without rotation policy or revocation hook.
severity_rules.description: TSC CC6.1 requires that authentication credentials are managed, rotated, and revocable.
severity_rules.remediation: Use short-lived tokens with rotation; implement a revocation list checked on every request.

severity_rules.pattern: Production-critical service has no uptime monitor, health-check endpoint, or alerting wired to an on-call rotation.
severity_rules.description: TSC A1.2 requires availability monitoring with documented response procedures.
severity_rules.remediation: Expose a `/healthz` endpoint; wire an external uptime monitor that pages the on-call rotation on failure.

severity_rules.pattern: Service stores critical data without a backup, replication, or documented recovery-time objective.
severity_rules.description: TSC A1.2 requires a documented recovery plan with backup integrity verification.
severity_rules.remediation: Configure scheduled backups, replicate to a secondary region, and document RTO/RPO targets.

severity_rules.pattern: API endpoint accepts user input without schema validation, type coercion, or boundary checks.
severity_rules.description: TSC PI1.1 requires that processing inputs are validated for completeness, accuracy, and authorisation.
severity_rules.remediation: Validate input against a schema (e.g., JSON Schema, Zod, Pydantic); reject malformed payloads with a structured error.

severity_rules.pattern: Error path swallows or logs-and-discards an exception during a financial or stateful operation, leaving partial state.
severity_rules.description: TSC PI1.1 requires error-handling that preserves processing integrity — partial-state failures must be detected and remediated.
severity_rules.remediation: Wrap stateful ops in transactions or sagas; surface failures with a typed error and a compensating-action hook.

severity_rules.pattern: Confidential data field (customer PII, internal credentials, financial records) persisted without classification or encryption-at-rest.
severity_rules.description: TSC C1.1 requires that confidential data is identified, classified, and protected via encryption.
severity_rules.remediation: Tag the field with a confidentiality classification; encrypt at rest with AES-256; document key custody.

severity_rules.pattern: Code collects personal data without a recorded consent flag or data-minimisation review.
severity_rules.description: TSC P3.1 requires that personal data is collected with consent and limited to what is needed for the purpose.
severity_rules.remediation: Record consent state at collection; review collected fields against the documented purpose.
